operation.title: Digital HDL build and simulation
operation.summary: Run a bounded compile, elaborate, execute, and collect workflow for an exact HDL source set while retaining independent step and self-check evidence.

assertion.statement: The exact declared HDL source set built and executed under the selected language rules, and fresh request-bound evidence proves the required deterministic self-check conclusion.
non_goals: Compilation or elaboration success alone does not establish design functionality.
A zero native exit status without the required self-check evidence is inconclusive.
This assertion does not establish synthesis, timing, physical, verification-closure, review, or signoff approval.
The operation does not mutate the authoritative HDL source set.

pass.meaning: Every required step completed in dependency order, required artifacts are fresh and bound to the exact sources, and the declared self-check conclusively passed.
required_evidence: Source and optional file-list SHA-256 identities match the canonical request.
Library preparation, compilation, elaboration, execution, and collection step records are complete and dependency-consistent.
The required assertion or deterministic self-check marker has fresh bounded evidence with status pass.
The executable and log lineage are fresh, contained, stable, and retained.

fail.meaning: Fresh request-bound diagnostics conclusively prove an HDL syntax, elaboration, assertion, or runtime design failure under the selected closed policy.
required_evidence: The failing step and its dependencies are identified without projecting later steps as successful.
Bounded diagnostics classify the failure as syntax, elaboration, assertion, or deterministic runtime failure.
The source lineage and relevant step log remain exact, fresh, and stable.

unknown.meaning: The workflow cannot justify pass or fail because it was unavailable, rejected, timed out, cancelled, ambiguous, stale, incomplete, over limit, or missing required self-check evidence.
required_evidence: The last truthful step state and bounded diagnostic reason are retained.
Missing, stale, partial, ambiguous, cancelled, or over-limit evidence is not promoted to pass or fail.

configuration_roles.description: An ordered source manifest whose regular-file members and order are bound by SHA-256.
An optional declarative file list whose exact bytes and resolved source order are both bound.

parameters_schema.title: Digital HDL build and simulation parameters v1alpha1

semantic_constraints: The source manifest is non-empty, ordered, contains only approved regular files, and binds each file's exact SHA-256 before any step starts.
An optional file list is declarative data only; includes, paths, and source order are resolved inside approved roots without shell or command text.
The declared top resolves to exactly one design unit; missing, duplicate, or ambiguous tops are rejected before compilation.
Only the closed compile-option vocabulary is accepted and requests cannot inject native arguments or actions.
Every step uses direct argv or a typed transport, inherits only host-approved context, and has independent timeout and cancellation handling.

data_schema.title: Digital HDL build and simulation normalized data v1alpha1

facts.description: Ordered bounded step records with explicit dependencies and independent execution and artifact states.
facts.bounds: At most five closed step records and 256 bounded diagnostic strings per step.

facts.description: The selected deterministic self-check policy, identity, conclusion, and evidence digest.
Exact source, file-list, executable, log, and optional waveform SHA-256 lineage.
Independent readiness, execution, artifact, engineering, review, and signoff dimensions.

integrity: Exact regular-file identities, ordering, sizes, and SHA-256 digests are stable through invocation.
artifact_roles.native_authority: The host-owned snapshot is authoritative for the attempted workflow.

integrity: The output is created in a fresh contained destination and hashed after stable close.
artifact_roles.native_authority: The selected implementation identifies the executable produced by elaboration.

integrity: Bounded stdout and stderr retain step identity, truncation state, and SHA-256 digest.
artifact_roles.native_authority: The typed step transport identifies the native diagnostic stream.

integrity: The self-check record is request-bound and cannot be inferred solely from process exit status.
artifact_roles.native_authority: The declared assertion or marker policy is the only authority for the self-check conclusion.

integrity: An advertised waveform is bounded, contained, stable, and SHA-256 identified.
artifact_roles.native_authority: The execution step identifies the optional native waveform output.

integrity_rules: Inputs are opened as regular files without following links and remain identity-stable through every dependent step.
Step outputs are fresh, contained, bounded, and hashed only after stable close.
Cancellation or timeout terminates only request-owned processes and retains truthful partial step records.
A zero exit status cannot substitute for the declared self-check evidence.
provenance_limitations: This profile binds direct source and generated-artifact lineage but does not prove semantic equivalence to another HDL implementation.
Workflow review and signoff remain explicit human-controlled dimensions.

features.request_effect: Select the closed IEEE 1364-2005 language surface.
features.conformance_case: Compile and self-check a bounded Verilog fixture and reject mismatched standards.

features.request_effect: Select the closed IEEE 1800-2017 or 1800-2023 language surface.
features.conformance_case: Compile assertions and reject unsupported SystemVerilog features deterministically.

features.request_effect: Select the closed IEEE 1076-2008 or 1076-2019 language surface.
features.conformance_case: Compile and self-check a bounded VHDL fixture and reject mismatched standards.

features.request_effect: Require fresh assertion evidence independent of process exit status.
features.conformance_case: Distinguish assertion pass, assertion failure, and zero-exit missing evidence.

features.request_effect: Request one optional bounded waveform artifact with exact lineage.
features.conformance_case: Retain a fresh contained waveform and reject stale, linked, or oversized output.

diagnostics.condition: The language, standard, top, option vocabulary, self-check, or source identity is invalid or ambiguous.
diagnostics.next_action: Correct the typed request; do not pass native options or command text.

diagnostics.condition: Fresh diagnostics conclusively identify a syntax or language-rule failure.
diagnostics.next_action: Review the exact bound source and selected language revision.

diagnostics.condition: The declared top cannot be elaborated or resolves ambiguously.
diagnostics.next_action: Correct the explicit top or source manifest without guessing another top.

diagnostics.condition: Fresh assertion evidence conclusively reports a design failure.
diagnostics.next_action: Inspect the bound assertion and waveform or log evidence.

diagnostics.condition: Execution completed without the required self-check evidence.
diagnostics.next_action: Report inconclusive and retain the execution evidence; never infer pass from exit status.

diagnostics.condition: A step timed out, was cancelled, was skipped, or has missing or stale artifacts.
diagnostics.next_action: Retain truthful partial records and clean up only request-owned processes and outputs.

semantic_bindings.implementation: Validate the exact source manifest, select only the Verilog standard pair, and execute the typed five-role fake pipeline.
Validate the exact source manifest, select only a declared SystemVerilog standard, and execute the typed five-role fake pipeline.
Emit a deterministic request-bound assertion record independently from the process exit status.
Create one fresh bounded fake waveform in the request-owned output root and bind its SHA-256 lineage.

native_actions: Resolve and snapshot the declared sources.
Prepare, compile, elaborate, execute, and collect through typed bounded steps.
Validate self-check and output lineage independently from native exit status.
limitations: This invented backend is a deterministic public conformance fixture and does not advertise native availability.

semantic_bindings.implementation: Validate the exact source manifest, select only a declared VHDL standard, and execute an unrelated typed fake pipeline with the same normalized step roles.
Emit an independently implemented deterministic request-bound assertion record.

native_actions: Resolve and snapshot the declared VHDL sources.
Prepare, analyze, elaborate, execute, and collect through typed bounded steps.
Validate assertion evidence and artifact lineage.
limitations: This invented backend is a deterministic public conformance fixture and does not advertise native availability.
The beta fixture does not implement optional waveform collection.

normative_surfaces: exact source and optional file-list identity
closed compile options and explicit top
independent readiness, execution, artifact, engineering, review, and signoff dimensions
fresh executable, log, self-check, and optional waveform lineage
compatibility.change_policy: Any change to accepted languages, standard pairing, option vocabulary, step roles, self-check semantics, result dimensions, evidence limits, or status meaning requires a new profile identifier.
